description: Figure 2 presents the equilibrium phase fraction diagram of Fe–10Mn–10Al–0.7C low-density steel calculated by Thermo-Calc software using the TCFE7 database. The graph plots temperature (°C) on the horizontal axis ranging from 600 to 1500 °C against phase fraction (0–1.0) on the vertical axis. Four distinct curves represent the equilibrium mass fractions of ferrite (α, BCC), austenite (γ, FCC), κ-carbide (κ), and liquid phase (L) as functions of temperature. The diagram reveals that ferrite (α) initially decreases with increasing temperature, reaching its minimum fraction around 1000 °C before increasing again. Austenite (γ) dominates the intermediate temperature range (800–1300 °C) with a maximum fraction of approximately 0.7. The κ-carbide appears below the eutectoid temperature of 823 °C via the reaction γ → α + κ, as indicated by the arrow. Liquid phase (L) formation begins above approximately 1350 °C. This thermodynamic calculation predicts that δ-ferrite forms first during solidification, followed by peritectic austenite formation, and provides the theoretical baseline for understanding κ-carbide dissolution behavior during annealing treatments.
